title: Your June Revenue Tells One Story. Your June Profit Tells Another.
linkedin:
Your June revenue numbers might be looking good. Your profit picture is a different story.

Most owner-led businesses head into EOFY focused on the top line. Revenue is up, the year felt busy, the books are about to close. But the revenue total in a P&L is a summary, and summaries hide things.

When you run a customer-level profitability analysis, three patterns almost always appear.

The first is the high-revenue client who quietly consumes more than they contribute. Extended payment terms, scope that drifts beyond what was priced, management time that adds up across the year.

The second is the smaller client generating disproportionate profit. Well-scoped, prompt payer, predictable needs. These relationships rarely get the attention their margin justifies because they do not dominate the revenue line.

The third is concentration. For most businesses, a small number of clients generate the majority of profit. That is a profitability insight and a risk question at the same time.

What makes June the right moment for this work is that twelve months of data are now available. The picture is complete. The decisions that follow from what you find, which clients to nurture, which relationships to reconsider, which service lines to protect, are the decisions that shape the entire year ahead.

Most businesses that run this exercise for the first time find at least one client relationship consuming considerably more than it returns. The revenue column looked fine. The margin column told a different story.

EOFY is not just a compliance event. It is the best data moment of the year.

Full piece on the ProfitPulse blog.

facebook:
Did you know your biggest clients by revenue are often not your most profitable?

As EOFY approaches, it is worth asking: where did your profit actually come from this year? Not just who invoiced the most, but which relationships left real margin after costs, time, and overhead.

For most owner-led businesses on the East Coast, the answer is usually surprising. The clients who generated the best returns are rarely at the top of the revenue ranking.

Our latest post breaks down how to find out which is which, and why June is the right moment to do it.

https://profit-pulse.com.au/blog-post

#ProfitabilityAdvice #AustralianBusiness
google:
June is the one month of the year when owner-led businesses have a complete picture of twelve months of trading. Most use it to finalise compliance work and close the books. The ones who get the most out of it use it to ask a sharper question: which clients actually drove your profit this year?

Revenue and profit do not always tell the same story. The customers who generated your strongest top-line numbers are often not the ones who contributed the most to margin. Some of the busiest relationships carry extended payment terms, scope that ran beyond the original price, or management overhead that erodes what looked like a reasonable return.

On the other side, most businesses have two or three smaller clients whose margin contribution is disproportionately strong. These relationships tend to be well-scoped, reliable, and easy to service. They rarely receive the attention they deserve because they do not dominate the revenue line.

A customer profitability review run now, while twelve months of data are available, gives you a clear basis for the decisions that shape the year ahead: which clients to prioritise, which service lines to protect, and where to focus your growth effort.

ProfitPulse works with owner-led businesses across Queensland, NSW, and Victoria to build exactly this picture at the point in the year when it matters most.

Read the full article on the ProfitPulse blog: https://profit-pulse.com.au/blog-post
